items.title: Temporal integration in human auditory cortex is predominantly yoked to absolute time
items.author: Nima Mesgarani
items.content: Temporal integration throughout the human auditory cortex is predominantly locked to absolute time and does not vary with the duration of speech structures such as phonemes or words.

items.title: Rapid learning of neural circuitry from holographic ensemble stimulation enabled by model-based compressed sensing
items.author: Liam Paninski
items.content: The authors develop a new computational system for high-throughput mapping of synaptic connectivity using two-photon holographic optogenetics and intracellular recordings.

items.title: High-throughput synaptic connectivity mapping using in vivo two-photon holographic optogenetics and compressive sensing
items.author: Valentina Emiliani
items.content: Using two-photon optogenetics, electrical recordings and sparse signal reconstruction, the authors demonstrate in vivo synaptic connectivity mapping in the mouse visual cortex.

items.title: Author Correction: Spatial transcriptomics reveals the distinct organization of mouse prefrontal cortex and neuronal subtypes regulating chronic pain
items.author: Yi Zhang
items.content: Author Correction: Spatial transcriptomics reveals the distinct organization of mouse prefrontal cortex and neuronal subtypes regulating chronic pain

items.title: Augmenting AMPA receptor signaling after spinal cord injury increases ependymal-derived neural stem/progenitor cell migration and promotes functional recovery
items.author: Michael G. Fehlings
items.content: Hachem et al. show that AMPAR signaling drives the acute activation of ependymal-derived neural stem/progenitor cells after spinal cord injury and that this mechanism can be targeted therapeutically to harness the endogenous regenerative potential of the spinal cord.

items.title: Recurrent pattern completion drives the neocortical representation of sensory inference
items.author: Hillel Adesnik
items.content: Neurons that respond emergently to illusory contours drive pattern completion in V1. Pattern completion in lower cortical areas may therefore mediate perceptual inference by selectively reinforcing activity patterns that match prior expectations.

items.title: Globally accessible platforms for the exchange of research findings and career development
items.author: Kuan-Lin Huang
items.content: Researchers’ access to scientific research findings remains inequitable owing to financial and geographical barriers, highlighting the need for more sustainable and inclusive modes of communication to complement existing journals and conferences. Free, online webinar platforms offer a powerful way to democratize scientific knowledge and support career development globally. Drawing from our experience building Open Box Science — a not-for-profit grassroots organization that has hosted over 250 webinars for audiences from more than 70 countries — and examining other successful initiatives, we share practical insights on how to build thriving communities for scientific exchange and call for the continuous support of such platforms. We also highlight how early-career scientists can leverage such platforms to expand their network and enrich their perspectives.

items.title: Grid cells accurately track movement during path integration-based navigation despite switching reference frames
items.author: Kevin Allen
items.content: Grid cells do not maintain a stable pattern during a self-motion-based task, but track animal movement in multiple local reference frames and reanchor to task-relevant objects, thus estimating local rather than global position.
